original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: Monash University and the International PCOS Network
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: International Evidence-based Guideline for the Assessment and Management of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome 2023
original_registry_entry.population: Adults and adolescents with PCOS or undergoing PCOS assessment, with recommendation-specific life-stage and diagnostic qualifiers.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: PCOS assessment, cardiometabolic screening, psychological review, shared decision-making, and management planning.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for selected PCOS identity, cycle, hyperandrogenism, cardiometabolic, and psychological-review documentation. Diagnosis, fertility planning, treatment, medicine selection, and UAE service pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Guideline overview and scope
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the international evidence-based PCOS assessment and management scope across relevant life stages.

exact_sections.heading: Irregular cycles and ovulatory dysfunction
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting menstrual-cycle pattern with life-stage and diagnostic qualifiers retained.

exact_sections.heading: Biochemical and clinical hyperandrogenism
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting acne, hirsutism, and clinician-reviewed biochemical hyperandrogenism assessment without asserting a diagnosis.

exact_sections.heading: Cardiovascular and diabetes risk
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports weight or BMI context, blood pressure, lipid profile, and glycaemic-status review in PCOS.

exact_sections.heading: Quality of life, depression, and anxiety
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting quality-of-life, mood, depression, and anxiety context without assigning a diagnosis.

title: International Evidence-based Guideline for the Assessment and Management of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome 2023
publisher: Monash University and the International PCOS Network
population: Adults and adolescents with PCOS or undergoing PCOS assessment, with recommendation-specific life-stage and diagnostic qualifiers.
intended_setting: PCOS assessment, cardiometabolic screening, psychological review, shared decision-making, and management planning.
access_limitations: Official host returned a challenge or shell page rather than guideline content.
